---
title: "Profile"
description: "Education, experience, research, leadership, awards, and selected coursework."
toc: true
---

:::::: {.page-hero .page-hero--compact}
::::: profile-hero-grid
:::: profile-hero-copy
::: eyebrow
Profile
:::

# Learn more about me.

Explore my educational background, research projects, and leadership activities.
::::

<img src="coffee_shop_wide.JPG" alt="Jett Palmer at a coffee shop" class="profile-portrait"/>
:::::
::::::

## About

I work at the intersection of statistics, AI, and organizational strategy. With a background in statistical consulting and collaboration, I use data to help teams define and navigate their toughest problems. My natural curiosity allows me to learn new domains quickly and implement *intentional* solutions that are grounded in the client's mission.

Maximizing **impact** and **engaging deeply** with clients is the central topic of my research. Learn more about the project [here](work/thesis/designed-for-impact.qmd).

## Education

::::: timeline
::: timeline-item
### California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo

**M.S. in Statistics**\
2025 - 2026
:::

::: timeline-item
### California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo

**B.S. in Statistics**\
2022 - 2025
:::
:::::

## Recent Experience

::::::::: {.card-grid .cards-3}
:::: work-card
::: card-kicker
Research & Consulting
:::

### Statistical Practice

- Principal Investigator, [*Designed For Impact: The Statistical Collaborator's Workflow*](work/thesis/designed-for-impact.qmd) (2025 - present).
- Lead Research Assistant, [*Authority Constructs in Data Science*](work/research/theobold-complex-instruction-pair-programming.qmd) (2023 - 2025).
- Consulted on projects in agriculture, environmental science, public health, engineering, and higher education.
::::

:::: work-card
::: card-kicker
Leadership
:::

### Strategic Initiatives

- Chaired a joint task force responsible for developing governance recommendations and advising 26 student leaders through an eight-figure merger of registered nonprofits.
- Facilitated cross-campus collaboration among student leaders and stakeholders, building consensus around a unified governance framework. Learn more about the project [here](work/leadership/maritime-integration.qmd).
::::

:::: work-card
::: card-kicker
Service
:::

### Community Contributions

- Appointed to over fifteen committees and governing bodies at Cal Poly to advise executive administrators on institutional decisions.
- Designed and hosted workshops for students and local nonprofits on data literacy and statistical tools.
::::
:::::::::

## Scholarship

### Publications

- Abrash, I., Canner, J., Geam, E., Kan, N., Kuebitz, C., Lopez, E., Palmer, J., Pfeiffer, I., Theobold, A., & Woodard, V. (Proceedings of the International Conference on Teaching Statistics, 2026). *Status, equity, and collaboration: A pilot study of Complex Instruction-informed pair programming*.

- Epstein, R., Newland, A., Palmer, J., Rajgarhia, A., & Wang, N. *Testing a quantitative social pressure theory of sexual orientation with data obtained from 310,144 participants in 92 countries completing surveys in their native languages*. Manuscript posted on SSRN, September 24, 2025. <https://ssrn.com/abstract=5526418>

- Chance, B., Kerr, A., & Palmer, J. (2024). Taking the next step in exploring the *Literary Digest* 1936 poll. *Journal of Statistics and Data Science Education*, *32*(4), 405-415. <https://doi.org/10.1080/26939169.2024.2395505>

### In Progress

- Palmer, J., Smith, H., & Vance, E. *Designed For Impact: The Statistical Collaborator's Workflow*. Learn more about the project [here](https://jettpalmer.github.io/designed-for-impact/).

### Conference Presentations

- Joint Statistical Meetings \| Contributed Paper Presentation (2026).
- e-Conference on Teaching Statistics \| Poster Presentation (2026).
- Bailey College of Science & Mathematics Research Conference \| Research Talk (2026).
- Western Psychological Association \| Paper Presentation (2025).
- e-Conference on Teaching Statistics \| Poster Presentation (2024).

## Leadership

### Governance Work

As an elected representative on the Board of Directors of a student association, I authored and passed legislation connected to institutional policy, shared governance, and student experience.

- Resolution on Institutional Growth and "Learn By Doing" Ethos ([Palmer & Ramage, 2026](https://www.asi.calpoly.edu/discover-asi/public-documents/agendas-minutes/board-of-directors-resolutions/)).
- Resolution to Amend ASI Articles of Incorporation ([Palmer, 2025](https://www.asi.calpoly.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Resolution-25-10-Amended-Articles-of-Incorporation_BODAppr-1.pdf)).
- Resolution Calling for Reform of the Course Evaluation Instrument and Processes ([Reyes & Palmer, 2025](https://www.asi.calpoly.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Resolution-25-04_Resolution-on-Course-Evaluations.pdf)).
- Resolution to Reaffirm Shared Governance ([Palmer & Finch, 2025](https://www.asi.calpoly.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Resolution25-03_Reaffirm-Shared-Governance.pdf)).
- Resolution to Recommend an Amendment to the Cal Poly Academic Senate Class Attendance Policy ([Palmer & Prince, 2024](https://www.asi.calpoly.edu/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/24-02-Resolution-to-Recommend-an-Amendment-to-the-Cal-Poly-Academic-Senate-Class-Attendance-Policy_BODAppr.pdf)).

### Selected Committees

- ASI Cal Maritime Integration Ad Hoc Committee, Chair (2025-2026).
- Artificial Intelligence Student Success Advisory Group (2025-2026).
- Distinguished Teaching Award Committee (2024-2026).
- Academic Senate: Student Perceptions of Teaching Effectiveness Ad Hoc Committee (2025-2026).
- Academic Senate and Academic Senate Executive Committee (2024-2025).
- Internal Review Committee, Chair (2023-2025).
- Campus Administrative Policy Committee (2023-2025).
- Student Success Fee Allocation Committee (2023-2024).

## Awards

- Champion Award, ASI Board of Directors (2026).
- Michael Koehn Scholarship (Finalist), Cal Poly University Housing (2026).
- Best Use of External Data, ASA DataFest at Cal Poly (2025).
- Kathy Storelli "Use Your Voice" Scholarship, ASI Student Government (2024).
- Best Visualization, ASA DataFest at Cal Poly (2024).
- Joyce Curry-Daly and James Daly Scholarship, Cal Poly Statistics Department (2023).
- Outstanding Character and Communication Scholarship, North County Education Fund (2022).

## Selected Coursework

::::::: coursework-grid
::: coursework-group
### Statistics

Advanced Statistical Computing with R; Advanced Design and Analysis of Experiments; Graduate Consulting Practicum; Statistical Machine Learning with R; Generalized Linear Models; Multilevel and Mixed Modeling; Statistical Consulting; Generalized Linear Models; Applied Multivariate Statistics; Survival Analysis Methods; Survey Sampling & Methodology; Statistical Communication; Statistics Education.
:::

::: coursework-group
### Computing & Data Science

Introduction to Data Science with Python; Statistical Computing with R; Statistical Computing with SAS; Introduction to Python; Data Structures.
:::

::: coursework-group
### Mathematical Foundations

Probability Theory; Estimation and Sampling Theory; Mathematical Statistics; Calculus I-IV; Linear Algebra; Methods of Proof.
:::

::: coursework-group
### Ethics & Philosophy

Meta Ethics; Science, Ethics, and Technology; Philosophy of Science.
:::
:::::::

## Resume

[Download Resume](jett-palmer-resume.pdf){.btn .btn-primary}
